phraseology.answer: Stop before entering or crossing the runway or taxiway.
distractors: Slow down before entering or crossing the runway or taxiway.
Stop only after entering and crossing the runway or taxiway.
Hold a short distance past the runway or taxiway hold line.
phraseology.explanation: A hold-short instruction means the aircraft must stop at the hold line and wait for another clearance.

phraseology.answer: Authorized to land on the assigned runway.
distractors: Authorized to approach but not yet to land on the assigned runway.
Authorized to taxi onto the assigned runway and land at pilot's discretion.
Authorized to land on any available runway, not just the assigned one.
phraseology.explanation: The aircraft may continue the approach and land on the runway named in the clearance.

phraseology.answer: Abort the landing and climb away for another attempt.
distractors: Continue the landing but circle the field after touchdown.
Abort the landing and taxi back around for another attempt.
Climb away from the runway after landing for a low approach.
phraseology.explanation: A go-around sends the aircraft back into the traffic pattern or missed approach flow.

phraseology.answer: Repeat the last transmission.
distractors: Transmit the last message to the next controller.
Repeat back the last transmission to confirm it was received.
Say the last transmission louder for clarity.
phraseology.explanation: This is the standard way to ask for a radio message to be repeated.

phraseology.answer: The transmission was received.
distractors: The transmission is approved.
The instruction was received and will be complied with.
Repeat the last transmission so it can be received.
phraseology.explanation: Roger confirms receipt, but it does not mean yes, approved, or cleared.

phraseology.answer: The instruction was received and will be complied with.
distractors: The instruction was received but cannot be complied with.
The instruction will be complied with after the next transmission.
The instruction was received; compliance is at pilot's discretion.
phraseology.explanation: Wilco is short for will comply.

phraseology.answer: Wait; more information or instructions will follow.
distractors: Wait on the current frequency and transmit a position report.
Stop transmitting permanently; no further instructions will follow.
Hold position on the ground until further information is provided.
phraseology.explanation: Stand by tells the pilot not to transmit again immediately unless needed.

phraseology.answer: Authorized to begin the takeoff roll.
distractors: Authorized to line up on the runway and wait for the takeoff roll.
Authorized to begin the takeoff roll once the preceding aircraft has landed.
Authorized to begin taxi to the runway for an imminent takeoff roll.
phraseology.explanation: This is the specific clearance that allows an aircraft to take off.

phraseology.answer: Yes.
distractors: Acknowledged.
Approved.
Maybe.
phraseology.explanation: Affirmative is the standard radio word for yes.

phraseology.answer: No.
distractors: Unable.
Disregard.
Not yet.
phraseology.explanation: Negative is the standard radio word for no.

phraseology.answer: Enter the runway and wait for takeoff clearance.
distractors: Enter the runway and begin the takeoff roll without further clearance.
Hold short of the runway and wait for takeoff clearance.
Enter the runway, line up, and expect immediate takeoff clearance shortly.
phraseology.explanation: The aircraft may position on the runway, but it is not cleared for takeoff yet.

phraseology.answer: Switch to the departure controller's frequency.
distractors: Monitor the departure controller's frequency without checking in.
Remain on tower frequency until the departure controller calls you.
Switch to the departure controller's frequency only after passing 1,000 feet.
phraseology.explanation: Contact means change frequency and establish communication with the named controller.

phraseology.answer: Set transponder code 4321.
distractors: Verify transponder code 4321 is already set.
Set transponder code 4321 only when airborne.
Read back transponder code 4321 without changing the current code.
phraseology.explanation: Squawk instructions tell the pilot which transponder code to enter.

phraseology.answer: Press the transponder ident button.
distractors: Identify the aircraft by callsign on the next transmission.
Press the transponder standby button to drop off the radar display.
Confirm the transponder code currently set in the aircraft.
phraseology.explanation: Ident highlights the aircraft on the controller's radar display.

phraseology.answer: Fly the runway's heading after departure.
distractors: Fly the runway's heading until reaching pattern altitude, then turn crosswind.
Track the extended centerline of the runway using GPS guidance.
Fly the reciprocal of the runway's heading after departure.
phraseology.explanation: The aircraft should continue straight out along the runway heading until instructed otherwise.

phraseology.answer: The pilot sees the referenced aircraft.
distractors: The pilot has the referenced traffic on TCAS but not visually.
The pilot is searching for the referenced aircraft and does not yet see it.
The pilot sees an aircraft but cannot confirm it is the referenced traffic.
phraseology.explanation: This tells ATC the pilot has visually acquired the traffic.

phraseology.answer: The pilot cannot comply with the instruction.
distractors: The pilot will comply with the instruction after a short delay.
The pilot did not understand the instruction and requests it again.
The pilot can comply with the instruction only partially.
phraseology.explanation: Unable is the concise standard response when a clearance or instruction cannot be followed.

phraseology.answer: Proceed straight to the named fix or waypoint.
distractors: Proceed via the previous route segments to the named fix or waypoint.
Proceed direct to the named fix or waypoint after crossing the next fix.
Proceed direct to the named fix or waypoint at pilot's discretion only.
phraseology.explanation: Direct means navigate to the specified point without following the previous route segments.

phraseology.prompt: Descend via the BIGGY THREE arrival, except maintain FL240
phraseology.answer: Follow the published vertical profile of the BIGGY THREE STAR, but do not descend below FL240 regardless of crossing restrictions.
distractors: Follow the published vertical profile of the BIGGY THREE STAR and descend through FL240 only when crossing restrictions require it.
Ignore the published crossing restrictions on the BIGGY THREE STAR and descend directly to FL240.
Follow the lateral path of the BIGGY THREE STAR but maintain FL240 throughout, disregarding all published speeds.
phraseology.explanation: Descend via authorizes the published vertical path; an except clause overrides the bottom altitude until further amendment.

phraseology.prompt: Cross LENDY at and maintain 11,000, Mach .78
phraseology.answer: Be level at 11,000 feet by the LENDY waypoint and fly Mach 0.78 from now on.
distractors: Cross LENDY at or above 11,000 feet and accelerate to Mach 0.78 after the fix.
Be level at 11,000 feet by LENDY and reduce to Mach 0.78 only when LENDY is crossed.
Be at or below 11,000 feet by LENDY, then climb back up while maintaining Mach 0.78.
phraseology.explanation: Crossing restrictions combine an altitude constraint at a fix with a speed assignment in Mach number for high-altitude ops.

phraseology.prompt: Hold east of WINDR on the 090 radial, left turns, 10-mile legs, EFC 1845Z
phraseology.answer: Enter a holding pattern east of WINDR along the 090 radial with left turns and 10-mile legs; expect further clearance at 18:45 UTC.
distractors: Hold west of WINDR along the 090 radial with right turns and 10-mile legs; expect further clearance at 18:45 UTC.
Enter a holding pattern east of WINDR along the 270 radial with left turns and 10-mile legs; expect further clearance at 18:45 UTC.
Enter a holding pattern east of WINDR along the 090 radial with left turns and 10-mile legs; clearance limit is 18:45 UTC and the aircraft must depart by then.
phraseology.explanation: A non-standard holding clearance specifies side, fix, inbound course or radial, turn direction, leg length, and the EFC time.

phraseology.answer: End any prior ATC speed restriction and operate at the company or planned speed schedule.
distractors: End any prior ATC speed restriction and accelerate to maximum operating speed immediately.
Maintain the previously assigned ATC speed restriction until reaching the next fix.
Resume the previously assigned ATC speed after a temporary reduction for spacing.
phraseology.explanation: Resume normal speed cancels previously issued speed assignments; the pilot reverts to nominal operating speed.

phraseology.prompt: Climb via the SID, except maintain 5,000
phraseology.answer: Follow all altitude and speed restrictions published on the departure procedure, but stop the climb at 5,000 feet.
distractors: Ignore all altitude restrictions on the SID and climb directly to 5,000 feet.
Follow all altitude and speed restrictions on the SID and continue to the published top altitude, expecting 5,000 next.
Follow the lateral path of the SID only, climbing freely to 5,000 feet without observing speed restrictions.
phraseology.explanation: Climb via authorizes published SID restrictions; the except clause caps the climb until ATC issues a higher altitude.

phraseology.answer: Pilot reports the aircraft cannot meet the required navigation performance and is asking ATC for radar vectors.
distractors: Pilot reports the aircraft can meet RNP but is requesting radar vectors anyway for traffic spacing.
Pilot reports loss of RNP capability and is asking ATC for an RNP approach to a different runway.
Pilot reports the aircraft cannot meet RNP and is requesting vectors clear of the approach for troubleshooting.
phraseology.explanation: RNP approaches require a specified containment value; if the FMS or GPS can't certify it, the crew must decline and request alternate guidance.

phraseology.answer: Slow to the lowest speed achievable without extending flaps or slats.
distractors: Slow to the minimum approach speed with flaps and slats fully extended.
Slow to the minimum maneuvering speed and extend flaps to the first setting.
Slow to the minimum speed at which the aircraft is still cleanly controllable in the current configuration.
phraseology.explanation: Minimum clean is the lowest maneuvering speed in the clean configuration; below it the crew would have to deploy high-lift devices.

phraseology.answer: Taxi onto runway 27L in line-up position and stop; the takeoff clearance has not been issued.
distractors: Taxi to the hold-short line of runway 27L and stop without entering the runway.
Taxi onto runway 27L, line up, and begin the takeoff roll without delay.
Taxi onto runway 27L in line-up position; takeoff clearance is implicitly issued.
phraseology.explanation: This is the older U.S. wording for line up and wait — still heard in legacy training material and on older recordings.

phraseology.answer: ATC is warning that the preceding departure was a heavy-category aircraft that may have produced significant wake; plan spacing or rotation timing accordingly.
distractors: ATC is informing the pilot that the preceding traffic was a light aircraft and will produce minimal wake on departure.
ATC is requiring the pilot to delay departure until wake turbulence from prior traffic dissipates.
ATC is advising that the pilot must produce reduced wake on departure to protect following traffic.
phraseology.explanation: Wake categories — Light, Medium, Heavy, Super — drive separation minima; heavies create the strongest wake on takeoff.

phraseology.prompt: Cleared for the approach, circle to land runway 33
phraseology.answer: Fly the published instrument approach to the primary runway, then maneuver visually within the circling area to land on runway 33.
distractors: Fly the published instrument approach to runway 33 directly without circling.
Fly the published instrument approach to the primary runway, then enter a holding circle before landing on runway 33.
Fly a visual circling pattern only, ignoring the published instrument approach, and land on runway 33.
phraseology.explanation: A circling approach uses the instrument procedure for descent, then a visual circuit at MDA to a different runway.

phraseology.answer: You may begin the descent to FL280 at any time of your choosing and at any rate; you may also level off at intermediate altitudes.
distractors: Begin the descent to FL280 immediately at the pilot's preferred rate; intermediate level-offs are not permitted.
You may descend below FL280 at your discretion, leveling off at any altitude of your choosing.
Begin the descent to FL280 when crossing the next fix; the timing is at the pilot's discretion afterward.
phraseology.explanation: Pilot's discretion gives the crew control of when and how to descend, unlike a standard descent clearance which begins immediately.

phraseology.answer: Call the named controller without ATC having coordinated a handoff in advance.
distractors: Call the named controller only after ATC has coordinated a handoff in advance.
Call the named controller without using your callsign on the initial transmission.
Call the named controller on the published frequency; no readback of the frequency is required.
phraseology.explanation: A free call is when the previous controller hasn't transferred radar identification, so the new controller doesn't yet know who you are.

phraseology.answer: Continue under visual meteorological conditions and accept responsibility for separating from the referenced traffic yourself.
distractors: Continue under visual meteorological conditions while ATC retains responsibility for separating you from the referenced traffic.
Maintain visual meteorological conditions and request separation from the referenced traffic when needed.
Continue under visual meteorological conditions and accept responsibility for separating from terrain along the route.
phraseology.explanation: ATC offloads separation duty to the pilot once VMC is reported — common in U.S. visual approach setups.

readback.prompt: Tower: Delta 482, cleared to land runway 27.
readback.answer: Cleared to land on runway 27.
distractors: Cleared for the approach to runway 27 but not yet cleared to land.
Cleared to taxi to runway 27 for departure.
Cleared to land on runway 22, not runway 27.
readback.explanation: The clearance authorizes landing on the runway named by tower.

readback.prompt: Ground: United 115, hold short runway 18.
readback.answer: Stop before crossing or entering runway 18.
distractors: Cross runway 18 without stopping and continue to the next taxiway.
Stop on runway 18 once you have entered it and await further instruction.
Stop before runway 36, the reciprocal of runway 18.
readback.explanation: Hold short means stop before entering or crossing the runway.

readback.prompt: Tower: JetBlue 64, go around.
readback.answer: Abandon the landing and climb away for another attempt.
distractors: Continue the landing but go around the aircraft ahead on the runway.
Circle the airport once at pattern altitude before being cleared to land.
Abandon the landing and divert to the alternate airport.
readback.explanation: Go around cancels the landing attempt.

readback.prompt: Departure: Southwest 22, contact center.
readback.answer: Switch frequency to the center controller.
distractors: Monitor the center frequency but remain with departure for now.
Stay on the departure frequency until center calls you.
Switch frequency to the tower controller, not center.
readback.explanation: Contact means change to the named controller.

readback.prompt: Tower: Alaska 9, cleared for takeoff runway 16L.
readback.answer: Cleared to begin the takeoff roll on runway 16L.
distractors: Line up on runway 16L and wait for takeoff clearance.
Cleared to begin the takeoff roll on runway 16R, not 16L.
Cleared to taxi onto runway 16L and hold short of the intersecting runway.
readback.explanation: Cleared for takeoff is the authorization to begin the takeoff roll.

readback.prompt: Ground: American 40, stand by.
readback.answer: Wait without transmitting until further instructions.
distractors: Stand by for an immediate taxi clearance and be ready to move.
Acknowledge ground and continue with the previous taxi instructions.
Switch to a standby ground frequency and wait for further calls.
readback.explanation: Stand by tells the pilot to wait; it is not a clearance.

readback.prompt: Tower: United 19, say again.
readback.answer: Repeat the last transmission for tower.
distractors: Tower asks the pilot to confirm the last transmission was received.
Tower asks the pilot to transmit their callsign again on this frequency.
Tower instructs the pilot to repeat the transmission to the next controller.
readback.explanation: Say again asks for the previous radio call to be repeated.

readback.prompt: Approach: Air Canada 5, traffic in sight?
readback.answer: ATC is asking whether the pilot can visually see the other aircraft.
distractors: ATC is reporting that the other aircraft has the traffic in sight.
ATC is asking whether the pilot has the traffic on TCAS, not visually.
ATC is informing the pilot that traffic is now in sight on radar.
readback.explanation: Traffic in sight means the pilot can visually identify the referenced traffic.

readback.prompt: Tower: Delta 77, wind calm, runway 04 cleared to land.
readback.answer: Cleared to land on runway 04 with calm wind reported.
distractors: Cleared for the approach to runway 04, hold landing clearance until wind picks up.
Cleared to land on runway 04 only if winds remain calm; otherwise go around.
Cleared to land on runway 22 because of calm wind, runway 04 not in use.
readback.explanation: Wind calm is information; cleared to land is the authorization.

readback.prompt: Ground: Southwest 8, taxi to runway 12.
readback.answer: Taxi toward runway 12.
distractors: Taxi onto runway 12 and hold for takeoff clearance.
Taxi to runway 30, the reciprocal end opposite runway 12.
Hold short of runway 12 at the current position until further notice.
readback.explanation: This taxi instruction names the runway the aircraft should taxi to.

readback.prompt: Ground: Speedbird 27, taxi to runway 09 via Alpha, hold short runway 15.
readback.answer: Taxi toward runway 09 via Alpha, but stop before runway 15.
distractors: Taxi toward runway 09 via Alpha and cross runway 15 without stopping.
Taxi toward runway 15 via Alpha, holding short of runway 09.
Taxi toward runway 09 via taxiway 15, holding short of Alpha.
readback.explanation: The taxi route is Alpha; the hold-short restriction applies at runway 15.

readback.prompt: Tower: American 310, line up and wait runway 22R.
readback.answer: Enter runway 22R and wait there; do not take off yet.
distractors: Enter runway 22R and begin the takeoff roll without delay.
Hold short of runway 22R and wait for line-up clearance.
Enter runway 22L instead of 22R and wait for takeoff clearance.
readback.explanation: Line up and wait is not a takeoff clearance.

readback.prompt: Departure: Lufthansa 441, climb and maintain one two thousand.
readback.answer: Climb to and maintain 12,000 feet.
distractors: Climb to and maintain 1,200 feet.
Climb to and maintain FL120, then expect 12,000 feet later.
Climb at 1,200 feet per minute and maintain present altitude.
readback.explanation: One two thousand means twelve thousand feet in this altitude instruction.

readback.prompt: Approach: Air Canada 83, turn left heading 270.
readback.answer: Turn left to fly heading 270 degrees.
distractors: Turn right to fly heading 270 degrees by the shortest direction.
Turn left 270 degrees from the current heading.
Turn left to intercept the 270 radial inbound.
readback.explanation: Heading assignments are compass headings in degrees.

readback.prompt: Tower: Emirates 204, wind 280 at 12, runway 27 cleared to land.
readback.answer: Wind is from 280 degrees at 12 knots, cleared to land on runway 27.
distractors: Wind is toward 280 degrees at 12 knots, cleared to land on runway 27.
Wind is from 280 degrees at 12 knots, cleared for the approach to runway 27.
Wind is from 280 degrees gusting 12 knots, cleared to land on runway 28.
readback.explanation: Wind information can accompany the landing clearance.

readback.prompt: Ground: Air France 7, squawk 4621.
readback.answer: Set transponder code 4621.
distractors: Verify transponder code 4621 is already set and report back.
Set transponder code 4621 only once airborne, not on the ground.
Set transponder code 4612, not 4621.
readback.explanation: Squawk assigns the aircraft's transponder code.

readback.prompt: Center: Speedbird 219, cross LENDY at and maintain FL240, then descend via the BIGGY THREE, Mach .80.
readback.answer: Be at FL240 by LENDY, then follow the BIGGY THREE arrival's descent profile, all at Mach 0.80.
distractors: Cross LENDY at any altitude above FL240, then continue descent on the BIGGY THREE arrival at Mach 0.80.
Be at FL240 by LENDY, then expect the BIGGY THREE arrival on next frequency, maintaining Mach 0.80 in the meantime.
Be at FL240 by LENDY, then follow the BIGGY THREE arrival profile but ignore Mach 0.80 once below FL240.
readback.explanation: A crossing restriction at the entry fix, then descend-via authorization for the rest of the STAR, with a speed assignment in Mach.

readback.prompt: Approach: Shamrock 104, cleared VOR runway 04 approach, maintain 4,000 until established, report procedure turn inbound.
readback.answer: Fly the VOR approach to runway 04, hold 4,000 feet until intercepting the final segment, and call when turning inbound from the procedure turn.
distractors: Fly the VOR approach to runway 04, descend to 4,000 once established, and report passing the procedure turn outbound.
Fly the VOR approach to runway 04, maintain 4,000 throughout the approach, and report procedure turn inbound only if requested.
Fly the VOR approach to runway 22, maintain 4,000 until established, and report when turning inbound from the procedure turn.
readback.explanation: A non-precision approach with an intermediate altitude floor and a position report tied to the procedure turn — three separate compliance items.

readback.prompt: Departure: Cactus 401, climb via the SID, except maintain 8,000, expect FL230 ten minutes after departure.
readback.answer: Follow all SID altitude and speed restrictions, level off at 8,000 feet, and anticipate FL230 about ten minutes after takeoff.
distractors: Ignore SID altitude restrictions and climb directly to 8,000 feet, then to FL230 in ten minutes.
Follow SID altitude and speed restrictions, climb to FL230 at the SID top, and expect 8,000 feet ten minutes after departure.
Follow SID restrictions but disregard speed limits, level at 8,000 feet, and climb to FL230 in ten minutes without further clearance.
readback.explanation: Climb via authorizes the published profile; the except cap overrides the SID top, and the expect clause is forecast information, not a clearance.

readback.prompt: Center: Singapore 25, hold east of ORION on the 090 radial, left turns, 15-mile legs, expect further clearance at 2310 Zulu, time now 2245.
readback.answer: Enter a holding pattern east of ORION along the 090 radial, left turns, 15-mile legs; expect further clearance at 23:10 UTC; current time is 22:45.
distractors: Enter a holding pattern east of ORION along the 090 radial, right turns, 15-mile legs; expect further clearance at 23:10 UTC; current time is 22:45.
Enter a holding pattern west of ORION along the 090 radial, left turns, 15 minutes per leg; expect further clearance at 23:10 UTC.
Enter a holding pattern east of ORION along the 090 radial, left turns, 15-mile legs; depart the hold at 23:10 UTC regardless of further clearance.
readback.explanation: A non-standard hold spelled out fully: side, fix, radial, turn direction, leg length, and EFC time, plus a current time check.

readback.prompt: Tower: Speedbird 88, cancel takeoff clearance, cancel takeoff clearance, hold position, traffic on the runway.
readback.answer: Reject the takeoff and stop where you are; the previous clearance is withdrawn because of conflicting traffic on the runway.
distractors: Continue the takeoff but expect to cancel the departure clearance after liftoff because of traffic on the runway.
Reject the takeoff, exit the runway at the next taxiway, and hold position because of traffic on the runway.
Reduce takeoff thrust and hold runway centerline; the takeoff clearance is reissued once traffic clears the runway.
readback.explanation: Doubled cancellation phrasing is used for emphasis on a safety-critical instruction; the reason is appended.

readback.prompt: Center: Norstar 71, when able direct ORION, then via flight-planned route, maintain FL360, Mach .82 or greater.
readback.answer: When you are able, proceed direct to ORION and then resume the filed route at FL360, flying at least Mach 0.82.
distractors: Proceed direct to ORION immediately, then via flight-planned route at FL360 holding Mach 0.82 exactly.
When able, proceed direct to ORION, then via flight-planned route, climb to FL360 once at Mach 0.82 or greater.
When able, proceed direct to ORION, hold at FL360 until further clearance, and reduce to Mach 0.82 or less.
readback.explanation: When able defers the routing change to crew capability; combined with a filed-route rejoin, an altitude, and a minimum Mach.

readback.prompt: Approach: Sunturk 233, vectors for the visual to runway 27R, follow the heavy Boeing seven-eight-seven five-mile final, caution wake turbulence, maintain visual separation.
readback.answer: Accept radar vectors to a visual approach for runway 27R behind a heavy 787 already on a five-mile final; you are responsible for spacing and wake separation.
distractors: Accept radar vectors to a visual approach for runway 27R ahead of the heavy 787 on a five-mile final; ATC will provide wake separation.
Accept radar vectors to the ILS approach for runway 27R behind a heavy 787 on a five-mile final; maintain visual separation only above the wake.
Accept radar vectors to a visual approach for runway 27L behind a heavy 787 on a five-mile final to 27R; maintain visual separation between the two.
readback.explanation: Visual approach with traffic to follow plus a transfer of separation responsibility to the crew under VMC.

readback.prompt: Tower: Channex 422, line up and wait runway 22L, traffic will land short, be ready immediate departure.
readback.answer: Enter runway 22L and hold; landing traffic will stop short of an intersection (LAHSO), and an immediate takeoff will be required when cleared.
distractors: Enter runway 22L and begin an immediate takeoff roll before the landing traffic touches down.
Hold short of runway 22L because traffic is landing on 22L; expect immediate departure once it clears.
Enter runway 22L and hold; landing traffic will roll out past your position, and a delayed departure should be expected.
readback.explanation: Line up and wait combined with land-and-hold-short context and a request for an immediate departure on subsequent clearance.

readback.prompt: Center: Aerolineas Argentinas 1304, unable higher due weight, request block FL340 to FL360.
readback.answer: The crew cannot climb above the current altitude because of aircraft weight and is asking for a block altitude clearance between FL340 and FL360.
distractors: The crew is unable to descend below FL360 due to weight and requests a hard altitude of FL340 once burned down.
The crew can climb higher but requests a block altitude of FL340 to FL360 to reduce fuel burn.
The crew reports unable RVSM between FL340 and FL360 due to weight and requests a non-RVSM block above FL360.
readback.explanation: A block altitude lets the aircraft fly anywhere within the band — typical for performance-limited cruise climbs or turbulence.

readback.prompt: Tower: Itarrow 7, go around, fly heading 250, climb and maintain 4,000, contact departure on 124.35, squawk 4621.
readback.answer: Abort the landing, turn to heading 250, climb to 4,000 feet, switch to departure on 124.35, and set transponder code 4621.
distractors: Abort the landing, turn to heading 250, climb to FL240, monitor departure on 124.35, and verify squawk 4621 is already set.
Continue the landing, plan a missed approach to heading 250 at 4,000 feet, contact departure on 124.35, and squawk 4621.
Abort the landing, turn to heading 205, climb to 4,000 feet, switch to departure on 134.25, and set transponder code 4612.
readback.explanation: A full go-around package: missed-approach heading and altitude, a frequency change, and a new squawk — five compliance items in one transmission.

compose.prompt: Tower: Delta 482, cleared to land runway 27.
answers: Cleared to land runway 27 Delta 482
Delta 482 cleared to land runway 27
compose.explanation: Read back the landing clearance and the runway, ending with your callsign. Either order (clearance-first or callsign-first) is accepted.

compose.prompt: Ground: United 115, hold short runway 18.
answers: Hold short runway 18 United 115
United 115 hold short runway 18
compose.explanation: Hold-short instructions are mandatory readbacks. Repeat the runway number and finish with your callsign.

compose.prompt: Tower: Alaska 9, cleared for takeoff runway 16L.
answers: Cleared for takeoff runway 16L Alaska 9
Alaska 9 cleared for takeoff runway 16L
compose.explanation: Takeoff clearance and runway are read back verbatim, then your callsign.

compose.prompt: Ground: Southwest 8, taxi to runway 12 via Alpha.
answers: Taxi runway 12 via Alpha Southwest 8
Taxi to runway 12 via Alpha Southwest 8
compose.explanation: Read back the destination runway and any taxi route assigned, then your callsign.

compose.prompt: Departure: JetBlue 64, climb and maintain 5,000.
answers: Climb and maintain 5000 JetBlue 64
Maintain 5000 JetBlue 64
compose.explanation: Altitude assignments require a readback. Either the full climb-and-maintain phrasing or the abbreviated maintain form is acceptable.

compose.prompt: Tower: American 40, contact ground 121.9.
answers: Ground 121.9 American 40
Contact ground 121.9 American 40
compose.explanation: Read back the new frequency with your callsign. The word "contact" can be omitted.

compose.prompt: Ground: Air Canada 5, squawk 4621.
answers: Squawk 4621 Air Canada 5
Air Canada 5 squawk 4621
compose.explanation: Always read back transponder code assignments to confirm the digits.

compose.prompt: Approach: Delta 77, turn left heading 270.
answers: Left heading 270 Delta 77
Turn left heading 270 Delta 77
compose.explanation: Read back the turn direction and the assigned heading, then callsign. "Turn" is optional.

compose.prompt: Approach: Southwest 22, reduce speed to 180 knots.
answers: Speed 180 Southwest 22
Reduce speed to 180 knots Southwest 22
compose.explanation: Read back the speed value with your callsign. Either the full or the abbreviated "speed 180" form is accepted.

compose.prompt: Tower: Air France 7, line up and wait runway 22R.
answers: Line up and wait runway 22R Air France 7
Air France 7 line up and wait runway 22R
compose.explanation: Line-up-and-wait clearances are mandatory readbacks because they put you on an active runway.

compose.prompt: Departure: Lufthansa 441, climb and maintain one two thousand, fly heading 080.
answers: Climb and maintain 12000 heading 080 Lufthansa 441
Maintain 12000 heading 080 Lufthansa 441
compose.explanation: Read back both clauses: altitude assignment plus heading, with callsign at the end.

compose.prompt: Ground: Speedbird 27, taxi to runway 09 via Alpha, hold short runway 15.
answers: Taxi runway 09 via Alpha hold short runway 15 Speedbird 27
Taxi to runway 09 via Alpha hold short runway 15 Speedbird 27
compose.explanation: Both the taxi route and any hold-short restriction are mandatory readback items.

compose.prompt: Approach: Emirates 204, descend and maintain 8,000, reduce speed to 250 knots.
answers: Descend and maintain 8000 speed 250 Emirates 204
Maintain 8000 speed 250 Emirates 204
compose.explanation: Altitude and speed are both mandatory readback items. Either full or abbreviated speed form works.

compose.prompt: Tower: American 310, line up and wait runway 22R, traffic on three-mile final.
answers: Line up and wait runway 22R American 310
compose.explanation: Read back the line-up-and-wait clearance and runway. Traffic information is informational and is not part of the required readback.

compose.prompt: Approach: Shamrock 52, cleared ILS runway 04 approach, maintain 3,000 until established.
answers: Cleared ILS runway 04 approach maintain 3000 until established Shamrock 52
compose.explanation: Read back the approach type, the runway, and any altitude restriction until established on the final segment.

compose.prompt: Tower: Delta 88, contact departure 124.35, squawk 4621.
answers: Departure 124.35 squawk 4621 Delta 88
Contact departure 124.35 squawk 4621 Delta 88
compose.explanation: Both frequency change and squawk code are mandatory readback items.

compose.prompt: Departure: Cactus 401, fly runway heading, climb and maintain 3,000.
answers: Runway heading climb and maintain 3000 Cactus 401
Fly runway heading climb and maintain 3000 Cactus 401
Runway heading maintain 3000 Cactus 401
compose.explanation: Read back both the heading instruction and the altitude. "Fly" is optional in the heading readback.

compose.prompt: Center: Itarrow 7, cross WINDR at and maintain FL240.
answers: Cross WINDR at FL240 Itarrow 7
Cross WINDR at and maintain FL240 Itarrow 7
compose.explanation: Crossing restrictions name the fix and the altitude. The "at and maintain" wording is optional in the readback.

compose.prompt: Tower: Channex 422, go around, fly heading 250, climb and maintain 4,000.
answers: Going around heading 250 climb 4000 Channex 422
Going around fly heading 250 climb and maintain 4000 Channex 422
compose.explanation: A go-around is read back as "going around" plus the missed-approach heading and altitude assigned by tower.

compose.prompt: Center: Norstar 71, climb FL360, proceed direct ORION.
answers: Climb FL360 direct ORION Norstar 71
compose.explanation: Read back the altitude and the assigned direct fix, ending with the callsign.

compose.prompt: Center: Speedbird 219, cross LENDY at and maintain FL240, then descend via the BIGGY THREE arrival, Mach .80.
answers: Cross LENDY at FL240 descend via the BIGGY THREE Mach 80 Speedbird 219
Cross LENDY at and maintain FL240 descend via the BIGGY THREE Mach 80 Speedbird 219
compose.explanation: Read back the crossing fix and altitude, the descend-via authorization (with arrival name), the Mach assignment, and your callsign. Other speed values and arrival names are decoys.

compose.prompt: Tower: Cactus 401, go around, fly heading 250, climb and maintain 4,000, contact departure 124.35.
answers: Going around heading 250 climb 4000 departure 124.35 Cactus 401
Going around fly heading 250 climb and maintain 4000 contact departure 124.35 Cactus 401
compose.explanation: All four items get read back: "going around", missed-approach heading, altitude, and the new frequency. Wrong frequencies and tower/ground are decoys.

compose.prompt: Approach: Shamrock 104, cleared RNAV runway 04 approach, maintain 4,000 until established, report procedure turn inbound.
answers: Cleared RNAV runway 04 approach maintain 4000 until established report procedure turn inbound Shamrock 104
Cleared RNAV 04 approach maintain 4000 until established report procedure turn inbound Shamrock 104
compose.explanation: Approach type, runway, altitude floor, and any reporting requirement are all read back. ILS and outbound are decoys.

compose.prompt: Center: Singapore 25, hold east of ORION on the 090 radial, left turns, EFC 2310 Zulu.
answers: Hold east of ORION 090 radial left turns EFC 2310 Zulu Singapore 25
Holding east of ORION 090 radial left turns expect further clearance 2310 Zulu Singapore 25
compose.explanation: A holding readback names side, fix, radial, turn direction, and EFC time, ending with callsign. Either "EFC" or the spelled-out "expect further clearance" is acceptable.

compose.prompt: Departure: Itarrow 7, climb via the SID, except maintain 8,000, expect FL230 in 10 minutes.
answers: Climb via the SID except maintain 8000 Itarrow 7
compose.explanation: The climb-via clearance is mandatory readback. The expect clause is informational and is not required in the readback per FAA convention.

compose.prompt: Center: Norstar 71, when able direct ORION, then via flight planned route, maintain FL360, Mach .82 or greater.
answers: When able direct ORION flight planned route FL360 Mach 82 or greater Norstar 71
compose.explanation: Read back the conditional direct, the route rejoin, the altitude, and the minimum Mach. Decoys flip the Mach restriction direction.

compose.prompt: Approach: Sunturk 233, vectors visual runway 27R, follow the heavy 787 five-mile final, maintain visual separation.
answers: Vectors visual runway 27R follow heavy 787 five mile final maintain visual separation Sunturk 233
compose.explanation: Visual approach with traffic to follow: read back the runway, the traffic to follow with wake category, and the assumption of visual separation.

compose.prompt: Tower: Channex 422, line up and wait runway 22L, traffic will land short, be ready immediate departure.
answers: Line up and wait runway 22L ready immediate Channex 422
Line up and wait runway 22L Channex 422
compose.explanation: LUAW and the runway are mandatory readback. Acknowledging "ready immediate" is good airmanship; the traffic info is not read back.

compose.prompt: Tower: Speedbird 88, cancel takeoff clearance, hold position.
answers: Cancel takeoff clearance holding position Speedbird 88
Cancelling takeoff clearance holding position Speedbird 88
compose.explanation: A cancelled takeoff clearance is safety-critical and gets a verbatim readback plus a confirmation that the aircraft is holding position.

compose.prompt: Approach: Endeavor 12, descend and maintain 6,000, reduce speed to 180 knots, expect ILS runway 25 approach.
answers: Descend and maintain 6000 speed 180 expect ILS 25 Endeavor 12
Maintain 6000 speed 180 expect ILS runway 25 Endeavor 12
compose.explanation: Altitude and speed are mandatory readback; the expect-approach line is also typically read back since it sets up the next clearance.
